name: Grotte du Mas-d'Azil and Lac de Mondély Loop
description: Cycle 22.1 miles (35.6 km) to Mondely Lake, a moderate road cycling route with 2200 feet (671 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Le Mas-D'Azil traverse a varied topography, featuring alternating slopes, hills, and ridge passages within the Ariège department of southwestern France. The region is characterized by the distinctive Plantaurel massif, through which the unique Grotte du Mas-d'Azil passes, and offers views towards the Pyrenees. Cyclists encounter a mix of paved surfaces, river valleys like the Arize, and natural features including lakes such as Lac de Mondély. Best road cycling routes around Le Mas-D'Azil The most popular road cycling route is Grotte du Mas-d'Azil and Lac de Mondély Loop, a 22.1 miles (35.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain and passes by a notable lake. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Col de la Crouzette (1245 m) – Col de Péguère loop from Le Mas-d'Azil, a difficult 55.6 miles (89.6 km) path. This challenging route includes substantial climbs over mountain passes, offering expansive views. Local road cyclists also love the Grotte du Mas-d'Azil – Cascades loop from Le Mas-d'Azil, a 32.6 miles (52.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain with waterfalls, often completed in about 2 hours 38 minutes. Road cycling around Le Mas-D'Azil is defined by varied topography, the Plantaurel massif, and views of the Pyrenees. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult climbs. The routes in Le Mas-D'Azil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Le Mas-D'Azil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Le Mas-D'Azil?
A: There are 15 road cycling routes available around Le Mas-D'Azil, offering a range of experiences for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Le Mas-D'Azil?
A: The region around Le Mas-D'Azil features varied topography, including alternating slopes, hills, and ridge passages within the Plantaurel massif. You can expect engaging cycling with both muscular climbs and fast, technical descents, primarily on paved surfaces. Many routes also offer views towards the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners or casual riders?
A: Yes, while many routes offer significant challenges, there is 1 easy route available. For a moderate option that still provides scenic views without extreme difficulty, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937618290/grotte-du-mas-d-azil-and-lac-de-mondely-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil and Lac de Mondély Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.6 km long with 670 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Le Mas-D'Azil offers 9 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36315424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de la Crouzette (1245 m) – Col de Péguère loop from Le Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt;, an 89.6 km path with substantial climbs over mountain passes, providing expansive views. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20540584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Mondély – Le Bascou loop from Le Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt;, covering 82.6 km with over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Le Mas-D'Azil?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Le Mas-D'Azil are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937618290/grotte-du-mas-d-azil-and-lac-de-mondely-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil and Lac de Mondély Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38707850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil – Cascades loop from Le Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes often pass by significant natural features. You can cycle through the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2204863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt;, a colossal cave traversable by road, and past picturesque lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2262154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Mondély&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38707850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil – Cascades loop from Le Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt;, even lead through varied terrain with waterfalls.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the region offers cultural and historical points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2204863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt; is not only a natural wonder but also a significant prehistoric site. The village of Le Mas-D'Azil itself has historical charm, and the area is home to several well-preserved dolmens, which can be integrated into your visit.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Le Mas-D'Azil?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling from spring through autumn. Summer offers warm weather, ideal for longer rides and enjoying the natural scenery. However, always check local weather forecasts, especially when planning rides with significant elevation changes or through mountainous areas like the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling options in Le Mas-D'Azil?
A: While many road cycling routes in Le Mas-D'Azil involve significant elevation, some sections or dedicated greenways might be more suitable for families. For instance, the area features highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2092636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;tunnels of Véloroute 81&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5152815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix–Saint-Girons Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which offer flatter, traffic-free paths ideal for family outings.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Le Mas-D'Azil?
A: Le Mas-D'Azil, as a central point for many routes, typically offers parking options within the village. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the individual route details on komoot, as many routes start and end in accessible locations with designated parking areas, especially near popular attractions like the Grotte du Mas-d'Azil.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Le Mas-D'Azil?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 17 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied topography, the unique experience of cycling through the Grotte du Mas-d'Azil, and the stunning views of the Pyrenees and rolling landscapes. The mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents also contributes to a highly engaging experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Mas d'Azil Cave?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate the famous Mas d'Azil Cave. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937618290/grotte-du-mas-d-azil-and-lac-de-mondely-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil and Lac de Mondély Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Another route that features this unique landmark is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10531206" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mas d'Azil Cave – Three Brothers Cave loop from Le Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt;, offering a 49.9 km ride.
tips: By this detour, you avoid going through the road that crosses the cavity right through. It is certainly one of the most beautiful views of this unique cave.
This dam lake is the ideal place to take a refreshment break, soak your legs, swim or even bivouac.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the To Mondely Lake road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is rated as moderate. It covers a distance of approximately 35.6 km with an elevation gain of about 670 meters, making it suitable for cyclists with some experience looking for a rewarding ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this route?
faqs.answer: The route features two notable highlights: the View of the Grotte du Mas-d'Azil, offering scenic vistas, and the tranquil Lac de Mondély, which is the destination of the tour.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Chemin du Piémont Pyrénéen, the Voie Verte Foix / Saint-Girons, and the Véloroute du Piémont Pyrénéen 06 (Saint-Girons - Foix). Smaller segments also follow Les terrasses de Souribet, Carrerot del Senyl, Rue du Presbytère, Chemin de la Plane, and Cami de Las Taychouneros.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle to Mondely Lake?
faqs.answer: Given its location, the best time to enjoy road cycling to Mondely Lake is typically during the spring, summer, and early autumn months. These seasons generally offer more favorable weather conditions, with less rain and more comfortable temperatures for cycling.

faqs.question: Is the To Mondely Lake route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is primarily a road cycling route, it is generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists directly on the road for safety reasons. If you plan to visit the lake area, please check local regulations regarding dogs around Lac de Mondély.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route or to access the Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas within the park. There are no external links provided for permits, so assume none are needed unless otherwise posted locally.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the start of this particular route is not detailed. We recommend checking local maps or komoot's route planner for designated parking areas near the starting point of the tour, which is typically in a village or town.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so you can expect paved surfaces. However, specific details about the road quality, such as smoothness or presence of potholes, are not available. Given the moderate difficulty and elevation changes, expect some climbs and descents on asphalt roads.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the way?
faqs.answer: While specific cafes or pubs directly on this route are not listed, the route likely passes through or near small villages where you might find opportunities for refreshments. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rides.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route or an out-and-back?
faqs.answer: Based on the tour data, this route is designed as a point-to-point or out-and-back journey to Lac de Mondély. For a circular experience, you would need to plan your return journey or explore connecting roads.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a road cycling trip to Mondely Lake?
faqs.answer: For a moderate road cycling route like this, essential items include a well-maintained road bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and navigation tools. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also recommended.
